message:
chore(beads): update br-3idj3 issue with release-ledger dashboard regression comment

Update the file_reservations active_only query regression issue (br-3idj3)
with a comment from GreenPlateau documenting the adjacent dashboard
regression found during the broader review: dashboard DB stats were counting
expired-but-unreleased reservations as active because the query used
ACTIVE_RESERVATION_PREDICATE without filtering by expires_ts > now. The
comment references the server regression test added in the accompanying
commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-3hsb","title":"Conformance: Add static resource query-param fixtures","description":"Add conformance fixtures for static resources without path params that accept optional query params (legacy URI templates like resource://projects{?format}).

Scope:
- Add fixture cases for:
  - resource://config/environment?format=json
  - resource://projects?format=json
  - resource://tooling/directory?format=json
  - resource://tooling/schemas?format=json
  - resource://tooling/metrics?format=json
  - resource://tooling/locks?format=json
- Update the legacy Python fixture generator to avoid duplicating format=json when the URI already includes it.
- Regenerate crates/mcp-agent-mail-conformance/tests/conformance/fixtures/python_reference.json and ensure cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-conformance passes.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyReef","created_at":"2026-02-07T01:23:20.203242500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T01:50:46.303770304Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T01:50:46.303745137Z","close_reason":"Completed conformance fixture parity and router/tool/resource alignment; cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-conformance passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-3i7fz","title":"R1.3: Implement format dispatcher — serialize any Serialize value to chosen OutputFormat","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:16:48.220987782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T04:43:30.506024687Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T04:43:30.506006173Z","close_reason":"format_output() and format_output_md() dispatchers already implemented in robot.rs — JSON/TOON/Markdown with MarkdownRenderable trait","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3i7fz","depends_on_id":"br-2gg5q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:05Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":812,"issue_id":"br-3i7fz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R1.3: Format Dispatcher

## What
A single function that takes a `RobotEnvelope<T>` and an `OutputFormat`, and produces the final output string. This is the central serialization gateway for ALL robot commands.

## Where
- Same module as OutputFormat/RobotEnvelope (robot.rs or lib.rs)

## Implementation
```rust
pub fn format_output<T: Serialize>(envelope: &RobotEnvelope<T>, format: OutputFormat) -> Result<String, CliError> {
    match format {
        OutputFormat::Json => {
            serde_json::to_string_pretty(envelope).map_err(|e| CliError::Format(e.to_string()))
        }
        OutputFormat::Toon => {
            let json_str = serde_json::to_string(envelope)?;
            toon::json_to_toon(&json_str).map_err(|e| CliError::Format(e.to_string()))
        }
        OutputFormat::Markdown => {
            // Markdown is command-specific — delegate to trait method
            // For generic case, fall back to TOON
            let json_str = serde_json::to_string(envelope)?;
            toon::json_to_toon(&json_str).map_err(|e| CliError::Format(e.to_string()))
        }
    }
}
```

## Markdown Strategy
Most commands use TOON/JSON. Only a few (thread, message, search) benefit from markdown.
For those, the command handler should implement a `to_markdown()` method on its data type,
and format_output should check for that trait before falling back.

Consider a trait:
```rust
pub trait MarkdownRenderable {
    fn to_markdown(&self, meta: &RobotMeta, alerts: &[RobotAlert], actions: &[String]) -> String;
}
```

## Key Decisions
- TOON via `toon::json_to_toon()` — serialize to JSON first, then convert
- This is slightly less efficient than direct TOON encoding but ensures correctness
- JSON pretty-printed for human readability when explicitly requested
- Error type: extend existing CliError with a Format variant if needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- format_output produces valid JSON when format=Json
- format_output produces valid TOON when format=Toon
- format_output handles markdown-capable types via trait
- Unit tests for all 3 format paths
","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:28:13Z"},{"id":813,"issue_id":"br-3i7fz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R1.3: Format Dispatcher

```rust
        }
        OutputFormat::Markdown => {
            // Markdown is command-specific — delegate to trait method

            let json_str = serde_json::to_string(envelope)?;
            toon::json_to_toon(&json_str).map_err(|e| CliError::Format(e.to_string()))
        }
    }
}
```

## Markdown Strategy
Most commands use TOON/JSON. Only a few (thread, message, search) benefit from markdown.
For those, the command handler should implement a `to_markdown()` method on its data type,
and format_output should check for that trait before falling back.

Consider a trait:
```rust
pub trait MarkdownRenderable {
    fn to_markdown(&self, meta: &RobotMeta, alerts: &[RobotAlert], actions: &[String]) -> String;
}
```

## Key Decisions
- TOON via `toon::json_to_toon()` — serialize to JSON first, then convert
- This is slightly less efficient than direct TOON encoding but ensures correctness
- JSON pretty-printed for human readability when explicitly requested
- Error type: extend existing CliError with a Format variant if needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- format_output produces valid JSON when format=Json

 {"id":"br-3ibsu","title":"T9.13: Port share/archive heavy suites to native E2E runner","description":"## Objective
Migrate share/archive shell E2E families to native runner without losing forensic artifact coverage.

## Scope
- Port share/export suites (share, share_verify_live, and related flows) to native am e2e run execution.
- Port archive suite (archive) with equivalent state and validation checks.
- Maintain deterministic artifact contracts and scenario repro metadata.

## Deliverable
Share/archive gates run natively under am e2e with parity evidence; shell wrappers remain optional compatibility adapters.","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope

Plan-space hardening additions:

- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"NavyOak","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:21:38.574530069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T18:54:28.255616465Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T18:54:28.255585708Z","close_reason":"Completed: strict native share+archive harness passes on rch; validations complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["archive","cli","e2e","migration","share"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3ibsu","depends_on_id":"br-dsdzo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:05Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":814,"issue_id":"br-3ibsu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-20 NavyOak progress: (1) DB-side regressions fixed in queries/schema for attachment policy preservation and inbox_stats trigger compatibility; added regression tests (, , ) and all pass via rch. Strict remote still failing; coordinating on agent-mail thread for rch/worker environment diagnosis.","created_at":"2026-02-20T18:22:53Z"},{"id":815,"issue_id":"br-3ibsu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-20 NavyOak clean update (supersedes previous malformed comment):

- DB-side regressions fixed in `queries.rs`/`schema.rs` for attachment-policy preservation and inbox_stats trigger compatibility.
- Added/updated regression tests:
  - `set_contact_policy_by_name_preserves_lookup_and_cache`
  - `register_agent_preserves_existing_attachment_policy_on_other_agent_upserts`
  - `inbox_stats_trigger_handles_repeated_recipient_deliveries`
- Verified via `rch`-run targeted tests: all pass.

Archive suite status:
- `rch exec -- env E2E_FORCE_BUILD=1 bash tests/e2e/test_archive.sh` passes.
- Recent local artifact evidence: `tests/artifacts/archive/20260220_180532` (Pass 16 / Fail 0).

Native harness status:
- Strict remote gate for `share_archive_harness` archive still failing on workers because archive adapter server never binds/opens port during startup.
- Mitigations already added:
  - configurable `E2E_SERVER_START_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` in `scripts/e2e_lib.sh`
  - default `AM_INTERFACE_MODE=mcp` in server startup helper for E2E
  - harness default env for startup timeout in `share_archive_harness.rs`
  - startup failure now emits server-log tail from `e2e_lib.sh`.
- Blocker remains remote worker startup behavior; coordinating via Agent Mail with rch/frankensqlite lane owner.
","created_at":"2026-02-20T18:23:06Z"},{"id":816,"issue_id":"br-3ibsu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"2026-02-20 NavyOak update (archive strict gate unblocked):

- Root cause found for strict remote archive harness failures: `scripts/e2e_lib.sh` defaulted `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` to `/data/tmp/cargo-target`, but some workers do not provide `/data/tmp`; server startup failed with:
  - `/data/projects/mcp_agent_mail_rust/scripts/e2e_lib.sh: line 2002: /data/tmp/cargo-target/debug/mcp-agent-mail: No such file or directory`
- Fixes landed:
  - `scripts/e2e_lib.sh`
    - default `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` now derives from `TMPDIR` (`${TMPDIR%/}/cargo-target`) and ensures dir creation.
    - `e2e_ensure_binary()` now validates executable path and falls back to `${E2E_PROJECT_ROOT}/target/debug/<bin>` if needed; explicit error logging when unresolved.
  - `scripts/e2e_test.sh`
    - default `TMPDIR` selection aligned (`/data/tmp` if present else `/tmp`), and `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` now derives from `TMPDIR`.
- Validation evidence (all via `rch`):
  - strict native archive harness now passes:
    - `rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/tmp AM_SHARE_ARCHIVE_HARNESS_SUITE=archive AM_E2E_SHARE_ARCHIVE_REQUIRE_PASS=1 E2E_FORCE_BUILD=1 E2E_SERVER_START_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=120 cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-cli --test share_archive_harness native_share_archive_transport_gate -- --nocapture`
    - result: `test native_share_archive_transport_gate ... ok` (finished in ~73.5s, remote exit 0).
  - formatting/check/lint gates:
    - `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` (pass)
    - `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` (pass)
    - `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` (pass)

This clears the remote startup blocker for archive strict-mode harness in this lane.

-{"id":"br-3idj3","title":"Fix file_reservations active_only query regression after release-ledger changes","description":"Observed on the live HTTP MCP server on 2026-03-09 while validating br-1591x. Reading resource://file_reservations/data-projects-mcp-agent-mail-rust?active_only=true returns DATABASE_ERROR with detail 'Query error: internal error: column not found: released_ts'. This appears distinct from the SQLite busy/resource-busy work: register_agent now correctly returns RESOURCE_BUSY under a real write lock, but reservation listing is broken. Likely area: aliasing/predicate rewrites around ACTIVE_RESERVATION_PREDICATE, file_reservation_releases joins, or resource query SQL that still references bare released_ts after the release-ledger changes. Objective: restore active reservation listing for resources/robot/CLI surfaces, add regression coverage, and keep release-ledger semantics correct.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:39:13.197137567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:39:52.564754804Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-3idj3","title":"Fix file_reservations active_only query regression after release-ledger changes","description":"Observed on the live HTTP MCP server on 2026-03-09 while validating br-1591x. Reading resource://file_reservations/data-projects-mcp-agent-mail-rust?active_only=true returns DATABASE_ERROR with detail 'Query error: internal error: column not found: released_ts'. This appears distinct from the SQLite busy/resource-busy work: register_agent now correctly returns RESOURCE_BUSY under a real write lock, but reservation listing is broken. Likely area: aliasing/predicate rewrites around ACTIVE_RESERVATION_PREDICATE, file_reservation_releases joins, or resource query SQL that still references bare released_ts after the release-ledger changes. Objective: restore active reservation listing for resources/robot/CLI surfaces, add regression coverage, and keep release-ledger semantics correct.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:39:13.197137567Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T02:01:34.514924084Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1169,"issue_id":"br-3idj3","author":"GreenPlateau","text":"Broader review found and patched an adjacent release-ledger regression in crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs: dashboard DB stats counted reservations with ACTIVE_RESERVATION_PREDICATE but omitted expires_ts > now, so expired-but-unreleased rows inflated active reservation totals and could trigger false dashboard/TUI anomalies. Added a focused server regression test covering active vs expired vs released reservations.","created_at":"2026-03-09T02:01:34Z"}]}

  - Beads: /dp/beads_rust
  - Agent detection: /dp/coding_agent_session_search
- No destructive commands or file deletions without explicit permission.

 {"id":"br-3jla","title":"T3.2: Implement direct DB seeding for benchmarks (bypass 62 CLI subprocess spawns)","description":"## Objective
Eliminate subprocess-heavy benchmark setup by implementing direct database seeding for benchmark scenarios.

## Work
- Replace CLI-loop seeding with native DB write paths for fixture preparation.
- Preserve dataset fidelity and determinism while dramatically reducing setup overhead.
- Validate isolation and cleanup behavior for repeated benchmark runs.

## Deliverable
A fast, native benchmark seeding path that removes the 62-process shell bottleneck.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RoseCave","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:43.701858971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T00:51:00.087659452Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T00:51:00.087637251Z","close_reason":"Completed: added native deterministic bench DB seeding API with reseed/idempotent behavior and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3jla","depends_on_id":"br-zxas","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:05Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":817,"issue_id":"br-3jla","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T3.2: Implement Direct DB Seeding for Benchmarks

## What to build
Seed a benchmark database with realistic data (project, agents, messages) using
direct DB calls instead of spawning 62 CLI subprocesses.

## Current bash behavior (bench_cli.sh lines 144-177)
```bash
# 62 subprocess spawns:
am agents register --project /tmp/bench --name BlueLake ...
am agents register --project /tmp/bench --name RedFox ...
for i in $(seq 1 49); do
    am mail send --project /tmp/bench --from BlueLake --to RedFox ...
done
for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    am mail send --project /tmp/bench --from RedFox --to BlueLake ...
done
```
This takes 3-5 seconds. Direct DB calls should take <50ms.

## Rust implementation
```rust
fn seed_bench_db(db_path: &Path) -> Result<(), Error> {
    let conn = open_db_sync(db_path)?;
    ensure_schema(&conn)?;

    // Create project
    let project_id = insert_project(&conn, "/tmp/bench")?;

    // Register agents
    let blue_lake_id = insert_agent(&conn, project_id, "BlueLake", "bench", "bench")?;
    let red_fox_id = insert_agent(&conn, project_id, "RedFox", "bench", "bench")?;

    // Send 50 messages BlueLake → RedFox
    for i in 0..50 {
        insert_message(&conn, project_id, blue_lake_id, red_fox_id,
            &format!("bench message {i}"), &format!("body of message {i}"))?;
    }

    // Send 10 replies RedFox → BlueLake
    for i in 0..10 {
        insert_message(&conn, project_id, red_fox_id, blue_lake_id,
            &format!("reply {i}"), &format!("reply body {i}"))?;
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

## Implementation notes
- Use the existing open_db_sync() pattern from the CLI crate
- Import schema creation from mcp-agent-mail-db
- Use transactions for bulk inserts (single commit = fastest)
- The seeded data should be identical to what bench_cli.sh produces
  (same project path, agent names, message subjects/bodies)
- Create a temp directory for the bench DB (cleaned up after benchmarks)

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/bench.rs (seed_bench_db function)

## Dependencies
- Needs the DB layer's insert functions (queries.rs or raw SQL)
- Uses sync SQLite (sqlmodel_sqlite), not async pool
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:31:06Z"},{"id":818,"issue_id":"br-3jla","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## CORRECTION from deep audit

DB seeding for operational benchmarks:
1. Create project at /tmp/bench via ensure_project
2. Register agents: BlueLake, RedFox
3. Send 50 messages from BlueLake to RedFox with varied subjects/bodies
4. Send 10 messages from RedFox to BlueLake (replies)
5. Total: 60 messages in the seeded DB

This seeding MUST happen via the am CLI or direct DB calls (not shell scripts).
Use the existing open_db_sync() pattern from the CLI crate.

The seed function should be idempotent — if /tmp/bench already has data, skip seeding.
Add a --reseed flag to force re-seeding.

 {"id":"br-3juna","title":"T11.1: Implement inspector overlay toggle and widget tree display","description":"Implement the F12 inspector overlay using frankentui's inspector module.

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. Add inspector_visible: bool to MailAppModel
2. F12 toggles inspector_visible (only when AM_TUI_DEBUG=true)
3. When visible, render inspector overlay at z-layer 7 (above help overlay)
4. Inspector shows widget tree with Rect positions and render timing
5. Arrow keys navigate the widget tree
6. Selected widget has its bounds highlighted with a contrasting border
7. Press Enter on a widget to show its properties (style, content snippet)
8. Escape or F12 again dismisses the inspector

GATING: Only available when AM_TUI_DEBUG=true. In production, F12 does nothing.
The AM_TUI_DEBUG check happens in the keybinding handler, not in the inspector itself.

FILES: tui_app.rs (F12 handler), tui_chrome.rs (inspector rendering)","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] F12 toggles inspector when AM_TUI_DEBUG=true
- [ ] F12 is no-op when AM_TUI_DEBUG is unset or false
- [ ] Widget tree displays all rendered widgets
- [ ] Selected widget boundary highlighted
- [ ] Escape dismisses inspector

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","notes":"Inspector implementation and unit tests are present in tui_app.rs/tui_chrome.rs.
